stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the continued industry interest in Meta's Llama models for generative AI tasks.

stories.title: From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI
stories.author: Shangding Gu
bullet_points: The paper identifies 'system scaling' as the next critical bottleneck in Agentic AI, moving beyond just model scaling.
It proposes 'scaling the harness,' treating the structured execution layer around foundation models as a primary design focus.
This shift emphasizes designing auditable, persistent, modular, and verifiable architectures for AI agents.

stories.title: From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI
stories.author: Shangding Gu
bullet_points: Identifies system scaling, rather than just model scaling, as the next major bottleneck in Agentic AI.
Proposes 'scaling the harness,' treating the structured execution layer around foundation models as a primary design focus.
Focuses on designing architectures that are auditable, persistent, modular, and verifiable for complex AI agents.

stories.title: DiscoverPhysics: Benchmarking LLMs for Out-of-the-Box Scientific Thinking
stories.author: Carolina Cuesta-Lázaro
bullet_points: Introduces DiscoverPhysics, a new benchmark designed to test genuine scientific reasoning in LLMs.
The benchmark challenges LLMs to discover the laws of motion in simulated worlds with non-standard physics (e.g., fractional-power gravity).
Addresses the critical issue of distinguishing between LLM 'reasoning' and mere 'recall' of existing scientific knowledge.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The tags confirm its association with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline shown is 'text-generation', indicating its primary application.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep
stories.author: Giulia Fanti
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability issue of attention mechanisms in LLMs for long-horizon tasks.
Proposes a 'sleep-like consolidation mechanism' to manage context length efficiently.
The mechanism involves periodically converting recent context into persistent fast weights via offline recurrent passes in SSM blocks.

stories.title: StakeBench: Evaluating Language Understanding Grounded in Market Commitment
stories.author: John Cartlidge
bullet_points: Introduces StakeBench, a novel evaluation framework for financial NLP.
It shifts focus from external labeling to grounding language understanding in actual market commitment.
Supervision is derived from observable market behavior across platforms like Polymarket and Manifold.

stories.title: Forgetting in Language Models: Capacity, Optimization, and Self-Generated Replay
stories.author: Andrew Gordon Wilson
bullet_points: Addresses catastrophic forgetting in language models, a known limitation where new learning degrades prior performance.
Proposes that self-generated samples from the model's own distribution can serve as effective replay data to mitigate forgetting.
Identifies model capacity as a key factor, noting that forgetting persists when the model has limited remaining capacity.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep
stories.author: Giulia Fanti
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability issue of attention mechanisms in LLMs for long-horizon tasks.
Proposes a 'sleep-like consolidation mechanism' to manage context length efficiently.
The mechanism involves periodically converting recent context into persistent fast weights via offline recurrent passes in SSM blocks.

stories.title: Automated Benchmark Auditing for AI Agents and Large Language Models
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Introduces Auto Benchmark Audit (ABA), an agentic framework designed to audit AI benchmarks.
ABA systematically identifies flaws in benchmarks, such as hidden dependencies and specification gaps.
Addresses the limitation of traditional verification methods failing to catch implicit assumptions in complex AI tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local image generation on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Google Health is here, but a lot of people want their Fitbit app back instead
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google has officially replaced the Fitbit app with Google Health.
The transition has caused significant user confusion and frustration among Fitbit users.
The change coincides with the launch of the new Fitbit Air device.

stories.title: American Airlines is getting Starlink Wi-Fi
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: American Airlines is integrating SpaceX's Starlink Wi-Fi into hundreds of its aircraft.
The deployment is scheduled to begin in Q1 2027 and cover over 500 planes, including new Airbus models.
Starlink will be added to existing in-flight connectivity providers like Viastat and SES.

stories.title: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: FBI is actively investigating the identification of individuals creating non-consensual AI pornography.
The investigation highlights the use of digital footprints, such as saved Instagram posts, to link users to illicit AI content.
The story addresses the growing legal and ethical challenges surrounding synthetic media and digital privacy.

stories.title: 3D-printable humanoid legs let robotics experiments run wild
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Hugging Face has launched a new $2,500 bipedal robot project.
The robot is designed to be used by builders and researchers for experimentation.
The project involves 3D-printable humanoid legs, enabling advanced robotics testing.

stories.title: Analyst on China's spent rocket stages: "Things only continue to get worse"
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: The article focuses on the danger posed by spent rocket stages as space debris.
An analyst suggests the situation regarding space debris, particularly from China, is deteriorating.
This highlights the growing environmental and operational risks in space infrastructure.

stories.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
stories.author: Zhaoxiang Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MobileGym, a browser-hosted simulation platform designed for mobile GUI agent research.
Provides verifiable outcome signals by using deterministic state-based judging over structured JSON states.
Enables scalable online Reinforcement Learning (RL) through low-cost, parallel rollouts without replicating proprietary backends.

stories.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of subject-driven image generation, aiming to synthesize new images while preserving a specific subject's identity.
Current methods often fail due to separate encoding of text and reference images, leading to poor cross-modal reasoning and artifacts.
The proposed solution conditions diffusion models to better integrate identity preservation with textual instructions, improving upon existing multimodal frameworks.

stories.title: Beyond Summaries: Structure-Aware Labeling of Code Changes with Large Language Models
stories.author: Karen Yorav
bullet_points: The paper addresses the growing challenge of manual code review due to large-scale software projects and AI code assistants.
It proposes a novel method for 'Structure-Aware Labeling' of code changes using Large Language Models (LLMs).
The goal is to automatically classify patch changes (e.g., renames, moves, logic modifications) to improve review efficiency.

stories.title: OrpQuant: Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for Multiplier-Free Power-of-Two Transformer Quantization
stories.author: Tao Luo
bullet_points: Addresses deployment constraints of LLMs/ViTs on edge devices due to memory and MAC bottlenecks.
Proposes 'OrpQuant,' a method using Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for quantization.
Focuses on ultra-low bit, Power-of-Two (PoT) quantization to replace costly MAC operations with bit-shifts.

stories.title: Channel-wise Vector Quantization
stories.author: Kaicheng Yu
bullet_points: Introduces Channel-wise Vector Quantization (CVQ) as a novel image tokenization paradigm.
CVQ replaces traditional patch-wise tokens by quantizing each channel of the feature map, representing images by visual detail levels.
The work proposes a new visual autoregressive framework built upon the CVQ concept.

stories.title: Claw-Anything: Benchmarking Always-On Personal Assistants with Broader Access to User's Digital World
stories.author: Dandan Tu
bullet_points: Introduces 'Claw-Anything,' a new benchmark for evaluating always-on personal assistant LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of current systems that only access narrow slices of a user's digital world.
Aims to capture performance in broad, context-sensitive, always-on assistant settings.

stories.title: VeriTrace: Evolving Mental Models for Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Alexandra Brintrup
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of managing vast, uncertain information in deep research AI agents.
Proposes moving beyond relying solely on LLM implicit reasoning for intermediate representations.
Argues for explicit regulation of the agent's 'mental model' to prevent error propagation from mixed-quality data.

stories.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of subject-driven image generation, aiming to synthesize new images while preserving a specific subject's identity.
Existing multimodal approaches often struggle with cross-modal reasoning and suffer from 'copy-paste artifacts' because text and reference images are encoded separately.
The proposed method conditions diffusion models to improve identity preservation, an area often overlooked by recent frameworks focused on instruction following.

stories.title: Prism: A Plug-in Reproducible Infrastructure for Scalable Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Prism,' a plug-in infrastructure designed to address engineering bottlenecks in Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT).
MCIT is a critical area of research enabling Large Language Models (MLLMs) to continuously adapt to new, emerging tasks in real-world deployment.
Current MCIT methods are limited because they require direct, complex modifications to the base MLLM codebase, which Prism aims to solve.

stories.title: Looped Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Dongmin Park
bullet_points: Introduces LoopMDM, a novel architecture for Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) in language modeling.
Proposes selectively looping early-middle transformer layers to enhance MDM performance and training efficiency.
Addresses the underexplored design challenges of transformer architectures specifically for MDMs.

stories.title: OrpQuant: Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for Multiplier-Free Power-of-Two Transformer Quantization
stories.author: Tao Luo
bullet_points: Addresses deployment constraints of LLMs/ViTs on edge devices due to memory and MAC bottlenecks.
Proposes 'OrpQuant,' a method using Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for quantization.
Focuses on ultra-low bit, Power-of-Two (PoT) quantization to replace MACs with hardware-efficient bit-shifts.

stories.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
stories.author: Zhaoxiang Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MobileGym, a browser-hosted simulation platform for mobile GUI agent research.
Enables verifiable outcomes via deterministic state-based judging using structured JSON.
Supports scalable online Reinforcement Learning (RL) through low-cost parallel rollouts.

stories.title: Prism: A Plug-in Reproducible Infrastructure for Scalable Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Prism,' a plug-in infrastructure designed to address engineering bottlenecks in Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT).
MCIT is crucial for allowing Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to continuously adapt to new, real-world tasks.
Current MCIT research is limited because existing methods require direct and complex modifications to the base MLLM codebase.

stories.title: StakeBench: Evaluating Language Understanding Grounded in Market Commitment
stories.author: John Cartlidge
bullet_points: Introduces StakeBench, a new evaluation framework for financial NLP.
Unlike existing benchmarks, StakeBench grounds language understanding in verifiable market commitments (e.g., Polymarket/Manifold data).
Supervision for the benchmark is derived directly from observable market behavior, linking comments to actual trading actions and odds.

stories.title: When Gradients Collide: Failure Modes of Multi-Objective Prompt Optimization for LLM Judges
stories.author: Abhishek Divekar
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of optimizing LLM judges for multiple evaluation criteria simultaneously.
Existing gradient-based optimization methods produce natural language critiques, preventing the direct application of standard multi-objective conflict-resolution toolkits (like PCGrad).
The research investigates five different decomposition modes for textual gradient optimizers to resolve these multi-objective conflicts.

stories.title: Peak-Then-Collapse and the Four Interface Channels of Knowledge-Graph Tool Use
stories.author: Dimitar Kazakov
bullet_points: The research investigates the stability of Reinforcement Learning (RL) agents using knowledge-graph tools (specifically on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct).
A key finding is the 'peak-then-collapse' pattern, where tool-grounded performance initially improves but subsequently fails completely.
The study identifies four recurring failure modes related to the interface channels when using a minimal knowledge-graph API.

stories.title: CausaLab: A Scalable Environment for Interactive Causal Discovery Toward AI Scientists
stories.author: Hao Peng
bullet_points: Introduces CausaLab, a new scalable environment designed for evaluating interactive causal discovery.
The environment specifically tests LLM agents on both solving problems using causal evidence and validating underlying causal hypotheses.
It simulates a synthetic laboratory setting where agents interact with physical systems (e.g., manipulating a crystal) to make predictions.

stories.title: Rosalind: A genomics toolkit in Rust running whole-genome pipelines on a laptop
stories.author: samuell
bullet_points: Rosalind is a new genomics toolkit written in Rust.
It is designed to run whole-genome pipelines efficiently.
A key feature is its ability to operate on standard laptop hardware.

stories.title: Cursor 3 ships parallel AI agents. Here is the multi-agent workflow that actually works.
stories.author: thegdsks
bullet_points: Cursor 3 introduces the capability for parallel AI agents.
The article details a functional multi-agent workflow within the new version.
The topic is highly relevant to developer tooling and AI integration in coding.

stories.title: Cómo Evaluar Agentes IA: Tutorial de LLM-as-Judge
stories.author: elizabethfuentes12
bullet_points: Introduces the 'LLM-as-Judge' methodology for evaluating AI agents.
Focuses on detecting critical issues like silent failures, wasted tokens, and hallucinations.
Provides a practical tutorial using Python code for pre-production testing.

stories.title: How I Escaped Claude & Cursor Limits: The Ultimate Free Local AI Coding Setup with Ollama + Continue.dev (2026 Guide)
stories.author: david_bilsonn
bullet_points: The article details a method for developers to bypass usage limits (e.g., Claude resets, Cursor costs) for AI coding tools.
It promotes a free, local AI coding setup utilizing Ollama and Continue.dev.
The guide is positioned as a resource for developers seeking self-hosted, cost-effective AI development environments.
